hero.headline: JAIN Online MBA in General Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) Online MBA with a General Management specialisation. NIRF #62 University, NAAC A++, UGC-DEB approved. Talent Acquisition, Consumer Behaviour, Investment Analysis, Market Research Tools, Strategic Management, and Organisation Development in Sem 3-4. Broadest career applicability across JAIN specialisations.

sections.tldr: JAIN Online MBA General Management: NIRF #62, NAAC A++, fees Rs 1.75L-1.96L (3 payment options), 4 scholarship categories. Cross-functional depth across HR, marketing, and finance subjects in Sem 3-4. Best for career changers, generalist management roles, and professionals who want breadth without a single-domain focus.

about.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in General Management is offered by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University), Bengaluru. JAIN holds NAAC A++ accreditation, NIRF #62 University ranking (2025), and NIRF #73 in Management. The online programme is UGC-DEB approved.

The General Management specialisation delivers cross-functional Sem 3-4 subjects spanning HR, marketing, and finance domains: Talent Acquisition and Management, Consumer Behaviour and Insights, Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management, Market Research Tools, Retail Marketing and Service Management, Organisation Development and Change Management, Indian Financial System, and Performance Management Systems.

This breadth is the defining feature of the General Management spec. Students gain working knowledge of HR, marketing, and financial management rather than deep specialisation in one domain. This suits professionals targeting general manager, business head, or operations manager roles where cross-functional management competence matters more than domain depth.

sem3Subjects.name: Business Research Methods
sem3Subjects.description: Apply both quantitative and qualitative research methodologies to business strategy and management decision-making.

sem3Subjects.name: Operations Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply process design, lean management, and quality frameworks to operations planning and general management roles.

sem3Subjects.name: Talent Acquisition and Management
sem3Subjects.description: Build talent strategy, manage employer branding, and design structured hiring processes for general management teams.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour and Insights
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse buying behaviour and cultural influences to inform cross-functional business strategy and brand positioning.

sem3Subjects.name: Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management
sem3Subjects.description: Apply DCF, relative valuation, and portfolio theory to corporate capital allocation and financial decision-making.

sem3Subjects.name: Market Research Tools
sem3Subjects.description: Apply SPSS, survey design, and secondary data analysis to generate market intelligence for business strategy decisions.

sem4Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem4Subjects.description: Develop competitive positioning, business unit strategy, and diversification frameworks for general management leadership roles.

sem4Subjects.name: Retail Marketing and Service Management
sem4Subjects.description: Manage category strategy, service quality design, and omnichannel execution for general managers in retail and services.

sem4Subjects.name: Organization Development and Change Management
sem4Subjects.description: Lead organisational transformation, manage change resistance, and apply OD diagnostic models to business restructuring.

sem4Subjects.name: Indian Financial System
sem4Subjects.description: Understand capital market instruments, RBI policy, banking regulations, and financial market structure for general management.

sem4Subjects.name: Performance Management Systems
sem4Subjects.description: Design cross-functional KPI frameworks, manage performance review cycles, and link business goals to team objectives.

sem4Subjects.name: Master Thesis / Project
sem4Subjects.description: Complete a general management research project on business strategy, organisational improvement, or market expansion.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 shared core subjects across all JAIN MBA specialisations. See the main JAIN Online MBA page for core subject list.

whoHires.body:
General Management MBA graduates from JAIN Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** IT services, FMCG, Retail, Financial services, Consulting, Manufacturing, Healthcare

**Entry-level roles:** Management Trainee, Business Analyst, Operations Executive, Assistant Manager (General)

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** General Manager, Business Unit Manager, Operations Manager, Branch Manager, Centre Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Business Head, COO, Country Head, MD (SME)

**Top hiring organisations:** Infosys Management Track, TCS Management, Deloitte Consulting, KPMG Advisory, Amazon Operations, Flipkart Operations, HDFC Bank Branch Management, Reliance Retail, D-Mart, Tata Group Management Trainee, Mahindra Group

skills.body:
The JAIN General Management specialisation builds cross-functional management skills that general manager and business head roles require:

**Technical skills:**
- Investment Analysis and Portfolio Management: corporate valuation, capital allocation frameworks, and financial statement analysis for general management financial decision-making
- Performance Management Systems: KPI framework design, cross-functional target setting, and performance review cycle management for general manager and business unit manager roles
- Organisation Development and Change Management: OD intervention design, change management programme leadership, and culture transformation for business restructuring roles
- Market Research Tools: SPSS and survey analysis for market intelligence generation and customer insights in cross-functional roles

**Applied skills:**
- Talent Acquisition and Management: team hiring strategy, employer branding, and talent pipeline management for general managers building and leading teams
- Retail Marketing and Service Management: category management, service quality design, and omnichannel execution for operations-facing general management

**Soft skills:** Managing cross-functional project teams, presenting business performance to P&L owners, negotiating with vendors and partners, and leading organisational change initiatives across functions.

comparisons.body:
Comparing JAIN Online MBA General Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA General Management:** Amity offers general management as an MBA track at Rs 2,25,000. Amity's all-India brand recognition is strong across sectors. JAIN General Management at Rs 1,75,000-1,96,000 covers HR, marketing, and finance subjects in the same programme - providing genuine cross-functional breadth. Python in Sem 2 core adds a data literacy layer Amity's general management track typically does not include.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA General Management:** NMIMS covers general management within its MBA structure. NMIMS carries stronger Mumbai corporate brand recognition. JAIN General Management's Bengaluru positioning gives stronger access to South India IT services and consulting general management tracks. JAIN's 7+ dual spec options also give students who later want a specialisation pivot a path forward.

**vs MUJ Online MBA General Management:** MUJ's general management track includes Coursera Plus electives for personalisation. JAIN General Management has a fixed cross-functional subject set covering HR, marketing, and finance domains in Sem 3-4. Both are comparable at similar fee levels. JAIN offers 3 payment options vs MUJ's semester-based payment.

fees.body:
The JAIN Online MBA in General Management is priced between Rs 1,75,000 and Rs 1,96,000 total program fee across 4 semesters (2-year program). JAIN offers different fee tiers based on specialisation - contact our counsellor for exact fee for this specific specialisation.

**Payment options (3 ways)**:

- **Semester-wise**: Rs 43,750 per semester (Tier 1) or Rs 49,000 per semester (Tier 2)
- **Yearly**: Rs 87,500 per year (Tier 1) or Rs 98,000 per year (Tier 2)
- **One-time upfront**: Rs 1,75,000 (Tier 1) or Rs 1,96,000 (Tier 2)

**Additional charges (Indian students)**:
- Registration fee: Rs 2,500 one-time
- Examination fee: Rs 3,000 per year (Rs 6,000 total for 2 years)

**Total effective cost (Indian student)**:
- Tier 1 specs: Rs 1,83,500
- Tier 2 specs: Rs 2,04,500

**International student fees**:
- SAARC countries: additional Rs 11,250 registration + Rs 3,750 per year exam fee
- Other international students: USD 150 registration + USD 50 per year exam fee

**EMI options**: Available through JAIN's direct NBFC lending partner tie-ups (same pattern as LPU and MUJ - not through external marketing partners). Contact our counsellor for current EMI tenure options, approval terms, and whether zero-cost or interest-bearing.

**Scholarships available** (4 categories - specific discount % and eligibility varies by admission cycle):
- **Merit-based**: Academic excellence scholarship for qualifying marks
- **Armed Forces**: Support for serving/veteran military personnel and families
- **Gender-focused**: Empowerment scholarship for diverse applicants
- **Freshers**: Transition support for recent undergraduates

Specific discount percentages are confirmed at admission based on eligibility. Contact our counsellor for current cycle scholarship details.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JAIN each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees, scholarship eligibility, and EMI terms with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The degree certificate is issued by JAIN (Deemed-to-be University) and carries the JAIN name with the MBA designation. The General Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode is identified per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The certificate is valid for corporate hiring, PSU eligibility, and higher education admission across India.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from JAIN Online MBA General Management alumni. Ratings from post-completion surveys.

items.name: Suhas Kulkarni
items.liked: The cross-functional subject mix across HR, marketing, and finance in Sem 3-4 was exactly what I needed as an operations manager. OD and Change Management was directly applicable to my team restructuring project.
items.disliked: General Management does not give deep domain credentials for specialist job applications. Best for generalist management roles only.
items.body: I had 6 years in IT operations management and needed a formal MBA credential. The cross-functional breadth of the General Management spec matched my role. JAIN brand opened doors at Bengaluru IT consulting firms.

items.name: Meera Subramanian
items.liked: Investment Analysis and Market Research subjects gave me financial and analytical foundations I lacked. Strong balance across domains.
items.disliked: Wished there was more tech sector-specific case study content given Bengaluru context.
items.body: Good programme for professionals targeting general management tracks at South India IT companies. Chennai consulting firms recognised the JAIN credential. Python in core curriculum was a genuine talking point in interviews.

items.name: Vikram Iyer
items.liked: Performance Management Systems and OD subjects were well-structured.
items.disliked: LMS quality was inconsistent. Some faculty for elective subjects lacked industry depth.
items.body: Decent credential for the fee. General Management is best for those sure they want a generalist career. If you have a clear domain preference, a specialised MBA from JAIN or a peer university gives stronger career positioning.

faqs.question: How are JAIN fees different for different specializations?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers two pricing tiers across its MBA specialisations. Tier 1 is Rs 1,75,000 total (plus Rs 2,500 registration and Rs 6,000 examination fees). Tier 2 is Rs 1,96,000 total (same additional charges). The tier applicable to the General Management specialisation should be confirmed with our counsellor as tier assignment can change across admission cycles.

faqs.question: What scholarships does JAIN offer for Online MBA?
faqs.answer: JAIN offers 4 scholarship categories: Merit-based (academic excellence), Armed Forces (military personnel and families), Gender-focused (diversity empowerment), and Freshers (recent graduates). Specific discount percentages and eligibility conditions are confirmed at admission and vary by intake cycle. Contact our counsellor for current cycle details.

faqs.question: Does JAIN offer dual specialization in Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. JAIN offers 7+ dual specialisation options covering combinations of Finance, Marketing, HR, and Business Analytics. Students considering General Management but wanting domain depth later can discuss switching to a dual spec during admission. Dual specs provide both breadth and domain expertise in a single programme.

faqs.question: Who is General Management MBA best suited for?
faqs.answer: General Management MBA is best suited for: professionals targeting business head, general manager, or operations manager roles where cross-functional management is required; career changers who want a broad management foundation before specialising; and entrepreneurs who need working knowledge of HR, marketing, and finance without deep domain credential.

faqs.question: Does General Management MBA cover HR, Marketing, and Finance?
faqs.answer: Yes. JAIN General Management includes subjects from all three domains in Sem 3-4: HR subjects (Talent Acquisition, Performance Management, OD and Change Management), Marketing subjects (Consumer Behaviour, Market Research, Retail Marketing), and Finance subjects (Investment Analysis, Indian Financial System). This cross-functional mix is the defining feature of the specialisation.

chooseIf: You want a cross-functional management credential covering HR, marketing, and finance from a NAAC A++ deemed university.
You are targeting general manager, operations manager, or business head roles where domain breadth matters more than depth.
You want 3 flexible payment options and 4 scholarship categories at fees of Rs 1.75L-1.96L.
consider: If a specific domain credential is required (Finance, HR, or Marketing), JAIN's dedicated specialisations or dual specialisations will carry stronger domain hiring recognition.
If pan-India general management brand recognition is the priority, Amity Online MBA General Management at Rs 2.25L is well recognised across India.
If a data analytics orientation is preferred, JAIN's Business Intelligence and Analytics or Data Science and AI specs build more quantitative general management skills.
